description_long: This object consists of two tall, upright stone grave markers set side by side on a rectangular stone base. Each marker is carved from gray stone, with the front faces featuring engraved inscriptions and decorative reliefs, including a lotus flower motif at the base of one. Small stone platforms at the foot of each marker hold cylindrical containers, likely for offerings or incense, and are surrounded by scattered leaves and debris. The rear sides of the markers are rough and unadorned, showing the natural texture of the stone. The markers are weathered, with visible chipping and erosion along the edges and tops. The overall appearance suggests a traditional, possibly East Asian, grave site commonly found in urban cemeteries.
description: A pair of tall, weathered stone grave markers with engraved fronts, decorative reliefs, and small offering platforms at their bases.
description_view_0: Front view showing engraved inscriptions, decorative reliefs, and offering platforms with containers at the base of each marker.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the rough, unadorned stone surfaces and the scattered leaves around the base.
description_view_2: Angled rear view highlighting the natural stone texture and the rectangular base connecting both markers.
description_view_3: Alternate front view emphasizing the engraved faces, lotus motif, and the arrangement of offering containers.
